audit_status.source_base: Hebrew Deuteronomy / Devarim Torah-guarding, Horeb revelation, anti-image, exile-return, and refuge-city discourse, draft aligned later
audit_status.review_note: First-pass draft reviewed for addition/subtraction warning, Baal Peor memory, wisdom among nations, Horeb without form, anti-idolatry, exile and return, Divine uniqueness, cities of refuge, and Torah introduction.

verses.restored: Now, Israel, listen to statutes and judgments I teach you to do, so you may live and enter and possess land YHWH Divine of your fathers is giving you.
You shall not add to word I command you, and you shall not diminish from it, to keep commands of YHWH your Divine that I command you.
Your eyes saw what YHWH did at Baal-peor, for every man who went after Baal-peor YHWH your Divine destroyed from your midst.
But you who clung to YHWH your Divine are all alive today.
See, I have taught you statutes and judgments as YHWH my Divine commanded me, to do thus in midst of land where you are entering to possess it.
Keep and do them, for this is your wisdom and understanding before eyes of peoples who hear all these statutes and say, Surely this great nation is a wise and understanding people.
For what great nation has a divine near to it as YHWH our Divine whenever we call to him?
And what great nation has righteous statutes and judgments like all this Torah/instruction I set before you today?

Only guard yourself and guard your soul/life greatly, lest you forget things your eyes saw and lest they depart from your heart all days of your life; make them known to your children and your children's children.

The day you stood before YHWH your Divine at Horeb, when YHWH said to me, Assemble people to me, and I will make them hear my words, that they may learn to fear/revere me all days they live on ground and may teach their children.
You drew near and stood below mountain, and mountain burned with fire to heart of heavens, darkness, cloud, and thick gloom.
YHWH spoke to you from midst of fire; voice of words you heard, but form you did not see—only voice.
He declared to you his covenant that he commanded you to do, ten words, and wrote them on two tablets of stone.
YHWH commanded me at that time to teach you statutes and judgments for you to do in land where you are crossing to possess it.
Guard your souls/lives greatly, for you saw no form on day YHWH spoke to you at Horeb from midst of fire,
lest you act corruptly and make for yourselves carved image, form of any figure, likeness of male or female,
likeness of any beast on earth, likeness of any winged bird flying in heavens,
likeness of anything creeping on ground, likeness of any fish in waters beneath earth;

and lest you lift eyes to heavens and see sun and moon and stars, all host of heavens, and be driven to bow to them and serve them, which YHWH your Divine apportioned to all peoples under all heavens.
But you YHWH took and brought out from iron furnace, from Egypt, to be for him a people of inheritance, as this day.
YHWH was angry with me because of your words and swore I would not cross Jordan and not enter good land YHWH your Divine is giving you as inheritance.
For I am dying in this land; I am not crossing Jordan. But you are crossing and shall possess this good land.
Guard yourselves lest you forget covenant of YHWH your Divine that he cut with you and make for yourselves carved image, likeness of anything YHWH your Divine commanded you against.
For YHWH your Divine is consuming fire, a jealous Divine.
When you father children and children's children and grow old in land, and act corruptly and make carved image, form of anything, and do evil in eyes of YHWH your Divine to provoke him,
I call heavens and earth as witnesses against you today that you shall surely perish quickly from land you cross Jordan to possess; you shall not lengthen days upon it, but shall surely be destroyed.
YHWH will scatter you among peoples, and you shall remain few in number among nations where YHWH drives you.
There you shall serve gods, work of human hands, wood and stone, which do not see or hear or eat or smell.
But from there you shall seek YHWH your Divine, and you shall find, when you seek him with all your heart and all your soul/life.
In your distress, when all these words find you in latter days, you shall return to YHWH your Divine and listen to his voice.
For a compassionate Divine is YHWH your Divine; he will not abandon you or destroy you or forget covenant of your fathers that he swore to them.

For ask, please, of former days before you, from day Divine created humanity upon earth, and from one end of heavens to other: has there been anything like this great thing, or has anything like it been heard?
Has a people heard voice of Divine speaking from midst of fire as you heard, and lived?

Or has a divine attempted to come take for itself a nation from midst of nation, by trials, signs, wonders, war, strong hand, outstretched arm, and great terrors, according to all YHWH your Divine did for you in Egypt before your eyes?
You were shown to know that YHWH, he is Divine; there is none besides him.
From heavens he made you hear his voice to discipline/instruct you, and upon earth he showed you his great fire, and his words you heard from midst of fire.
Because he loved your fathers and chose their seed after them, he brought you out from Egypt by his presence and great power,
to dispossess nations greater and mightier than you from before you, to bring you in, to give you their land as inheritance, as this day.
Know today and return to your heart that YHWH, he is Divine in heavens above and upon earth beneath; there is none else.
Keep his statutes and commands that I command you today, so it may be well with you and your children after you, and so you may lengthen days upon ground YHWH your Divine gives you all days.
Then Moses separated three cities across Jordan toward sunrise,
for manslayer to flee there who strikes his neighbor unintentionally and did not hate him in time past; he may flee to one of these cities and live:
Bezer in wilderness on plateau for Reubenites, Ramoth in Gilead for Gadites, and Golan in Bashan for Manassites.
This is Torah/instruction that Moses set before children of Israel.
These are testimonies and statutes and judgments Moses spoke to children of Israel when they came out from Egypt,
across Jordan in valley opposite Beth-peor, in land of Sihon king of Amorite dwelling in Heshbon, whom Moses and children of Israel struck when they came out from Egypt.
They possessed his land and land of Og king of Bashan, two kings of Amorite across Jordan toward sunrise,
from Aroer on edge of wadi Arnon to Mount Sion, that is Hermon,
and all Arabah across Jordan eastward, as far as sea of Arabah below slopes of Pisgah.

translation_notes: Horeb revelation is emphatically a heard voice without visible form; anti-image language protects Divine transcendence but must not justify destruction of other people's art or sacred sites.
Israel's Torah is presented as wisdom visible to nations, not as permission for ethnic supremacy.
The exile warning ends in return, seeking, compassion, and covenant memory—not permanent rejection.
Claims of Divine uniqueness and Israel's election remain Jewish covenant language and must not be superseded by later communities.
Verse 38's dispossession language remains morally contested and cannot authorize modern territorial violence.
The cities of refuge insert life-preserving asylum into the chapter's covenant exhortation.
safety_note: Do not use Deuteronomy 4 to justify iconoclasm, forced conversion, antisemitism, supersessionism, colonial dispossession, or blaming exiled and traumatized peoples for their suffering. Center memory, compassionate return, Torah wisdom, and refuge from revenge.
